data.title: Turkey detains lawyer of jailed Istanbul mayor, main opposition party says
data.description:
Turkish authorities detained a lawyer for President Tayyip Erdogan's biggest political rival, jailed Istanbul Mayor Ekrem Imamoglu, an MP of the main opposition party, CHP, said on Friday.

Imamoglu, a CHP member who leads Erdoguan in some polls, was jailed on Sunday pending trial for graft, provoking the largest anti-government protests in a decade, which led in turn to mass arrests nationwide.

Mehmet Pehlivan, a lawyer who defended Imamoglu in the latest investigation, was detained "for fabricated reasons", CHP MP Turan Taskin Ozer said in a post on X, without giving details.

Imamoglu, who has been temporarily dismissed from his job because of the case, demanded the immediate release of his lawyer.

"As if the coup on democracy wasn't enough, they can't stand the victims of this coup defending themselves," Imamoglu said on X.

data.title: Hexagon Purus Books Order for Hydrogen Fuel Storage Systems from Stadler Rail
data.description:
Hexagon Purus said Thursday it received an order for hydrogen fuel storage systems from rail applications manufacturer Stadler Rail .

The multi-year deal concerns the Norwegian mobility and infrastructure company's type 4 hydrogen fuel storage systems that it intends to produce from its site in Kassel, Germany. The systems will be used for hydrogen rail projects in the US state of California.

data.title: Stovec Industries CFO Resigns; Shares Slip 1%
data.description:
Stovec Industries said Paras Mehta has resigned as chief financial officer and key managerial person of the company, according to an Indian bourse filing on Thursday.

Mehta resigned to seek opportunity outside the company, the filing said.

Shares of the company were down 0.8% in early trading.

data.title: Amoytop Biotech 2024 Profit Up 49%, Revenue Rises 34%
data.description:
Xiamen Amoytop Biotech posted 2024 net profit attributable to shareholders of 827.6 million yuan, up 49% from 555.4 million yuan a year earlier.

Earnings per share climbed 48% year over year to 2.03 yuan from 1.37 yuan, according to a filing with the Shanghai bourse.

The biotech company's revenue rose 34% to 2.82 billion yuan from 2.10 billion yuan.

Xiamen Amoytop Biotech declared a 2024 dividend of 0.62 yuan per share.

Shares of the company rose 1% in recent trade.

data.title: New Zealand Shares Fall Amid Rising Global Trade War Fears
data.description:
New Zealand shares saw another session of losses on Friday, along with other Asian indexes, as markets continued to digest an uncertain global outlook because of US tariffs.

The S&P/NZX 50 Index fell 0.2% or 18.33 points to close at 12,287.46.

US President Donald Trump announced plans to impose a 25% tariff on all foreign-made vehicles and auto parts, a move that has led companies like Volvo and Hyundai to relocate some of their production to the US.

Meanwhile, continued global business outlook fears sent gold to yet another record high.

On the local front, New Zealand's consumer confidence fell in March, with declines across nearly all components, according to a survey by ANZ Research and Roy Morgan Research released Friday.

Also, the number of enterprise entries in New Zealand rose to 4,167 in February, compared with 3,084 in January, Stats NZ data showed Friday.

New Zealand's seasonally adjusted filled jobs across all industries were flat at nearly 2.4 million in February, following a 0.1% uptick in the prior month, Stats NZ data showed Friday.

In corporate news, Hallenstein Glasson Holdings reported Friday fiscal half-year earnings of NZ$0.355 per diluted share, unchanged from the year-earlier period. The company's shares edged up at market close.

Sky Network Television secured an eight-year exclusive broadcast right to air Six Nations and Gallagher Premiership Rugby in New Zealand. The company's Kiwi shares finished modestly higher.

data.title: Singapore's Import, Export Prices Fall in February
data.description:
Singapore's Import Price Index fell 3.3% year on year in February, extending the 2.2% decrease in the preceding month, the Department of Statistics reported Friday.

The Non-Oil Index was down 3.2%, while the Oil Index dropped 3.7%.

The Export Price Index also slipped 3.3% year on year in February, following a 2.2% decrease in the previous month. The non-oil index slid 2.4%, while the oil index dipped 7.1% on year.

On a month-on-month basis, the Import Price Index fell 1.5% in February, reversing a 0.8% increase a month before, while the Export Price Index was down 0.5% during the month, after a 0.8% increase in the previous month, the report said.

data.title: IT, auto drag India's benchmarks as tariff worries weigh
data.description:
Indian benchmark indexes fell on Friday, dragged by IT and auto stocks as uncertainty over U.S. reciprocal tariffs that are to be announced next week made investors cautious.

The NSE Nifty 50 was down 0.17% to 23,553.8, while the BSE Sensex traded 0.20% lower at 77,451 as of 10:06 a.m. IST.

Other Asian markets fell, dragged by auto stocks, after U.S. President Donald Trump announced a 25% tariff on automotive imports, due to kick in next week.

"The market will remain subdued in the next couple of sessions due to uncertainties surrounding the U.S. tariffs, which will influence the immediate trajectory and keep investors cautious," said Sameet Chavan, head of research at Angel One.

Trump is expected to implement retaliatory tariffs on April 2 but the U.S. administration has indicated that not all tariffs will be levied.

Indian auto shares lost 0.8% on the day, taking their two-day decline to 2%.

Pharma stocks fell 0.9%, also weighed down by tariff worries. The U.S. accounts for about one-third of India's total pharmaceutical exports.

Information technology companies , which earn a major share of their revenue from the U.S., lost 1.3% ahead of a key U.S. inflation reading due after Indian market hours.

Eight of the ten Nifty IT constituents declined. Tata Consultancy Services and Infosys fell 1.1% and 1.7%, respectively.

Bucking the trend, exchange operator BSE surged 12% after the country's markets regulator rejigged the derivatives expiry schedule, a move analysts said will help ease concerns over the bourse's market share loss.

The broader, more domestically focussed smallcaps and midcaps gained 0.6% and 0.4%, respectively.

($1 = 85.6830 Indian rupees)

data.title: New Zealand Shares Lose 0.15%, But Snap Run of Weekly Losses — Market Talk
data.description: New Zealand's NZX-50 closed 0.15% lower at 12287.46 under the weight of its largest five stocks, but still snapped a run of five consecutive weekly losses. The benchmark index spent Friday's session bouncing between small gains and losses, before finishing in the red amid retreats by Infratil, Meridian Energy, Auckland International Airport, Mercury NZ and Fisher & Paykel. Medical-device maker Fisher & Paykel was the biggest loser of the group, shedding 1.5% amid continued worries over tariffs' impact on global trade. Elsewhere, logistics operators Mainfreight and Freightways gave up 1.6% and 1.9%, respectively. The NZX-50 rose 1.4% for the week. (stuart.condie@wsj.com)

data.title: Li Ning's 2024 Profit Slides About 6%; Shares Rise 3%
data.description:
Li Ning's attributable profit fell 5.5% to 3.01 billion yuan in 2024 from 3.19 billion yuan in 2023, according to a Friday filing with the Hong Kong bourse.

Earnings per share at the sports apparel maker decreased to 1.1652 yuan from 1.2266 yuan a year earlier. Analysts at Visible Alpha estimated EPS at 1.19 yuan.

Revenue grew 3.9% to 28.7 billion yuan from 27.6 billion yuan in the previous year. Visible Alpha analysts forecast revenue at 28.2 billion yuan.

The company declared a final dividend of 0.2073 yuan per share, payable June 27 to shareholders on record as of June 19.

Shares jumped 3% during Friday's late-morning trading.

data.title: Hyundai Motor Set to Take Substantial Earnings Hit From Trump's Tariff — Market Talk
data.description: Hyundai Motor is set to take a substantial earnings hit from President Trump's 25% auto tariff and a likely 5% average-selling-price increase to ease the tariff impact for cars selling in the U.S., Nomura analyst Angela Hong writes in a note. Hong lowers her 2025 operating-profit forecast for the South Korean carmaker by 18%, noting Hyundai's 48% reliance on imports for vehicle sales in the U.S. She also revises down her 2025 EPS for Hyundai by 13%. Nomura cuts its target price for the stock by 11% to KRW250,000 but maintains a buy rating, noting that investors have priced in much of the tariff impact. Shares are 4.2% lower at KRW203,500.(kwanwoo.jun@wsj.com)

data.title: Levima Advanced Materials' Profit Drops 47%, Operating Income Slips 8%; Shares Down 3%
data.description:
Levima Advanced Materials' net profit attributable to shareholders in 2024 dropped 47% to 234.4 million yuan from 446.1 million yuan a year earlier, according to a Shenzhen Stock Exchange filing on Friday.

Earnings per share fell 45% year on year to 0.18 yuan from 0.33 yuan.

The Chinese polymer materials and specialty chemicals manufacturer's operating income slipped 7.5% to 6.27 billion yuan from 6.78 billion yuan in the previous year.

The company's shares dropped over 3% in recent trade.

data.title: Kering Completes Sale of 60% Stake in Paris Portfolio to Ardian
data.description:
Kering said Thursday it completed the divestment of a 60% stake in a three-property portfolio in Paris to French private equity investor Ardian.

The deal covers the Hôtel de Nocé and two buildings on avenue Montaigne. The French luxury goods company agreed to sell a majority of the portfolio earlier in 2025, expecting to generate 837 million euros in net proceeds from the transaction while retaining a 40% stake in the properties.

data.title: Toyota's global output rises for second month in February on solid Japan sales
data.description:
Key points:
  • Toyota's global production, sales rose 6% y/y in February
  • Domestic production up 16%, overseas output less than 1% higher
  • Sales in Japan rise 28%, overseas sales up 2%

Toyota Motor said on Friday its global production rose for the second consecutive month in February, driven by higher output and stronger sales in Japan after recovering from a certification scandal in its home market last year.

Global output for the world's biggest automaker increased 6% to 779,790 vehicles year-on-year, with domestic production up 16% and that in other countries growing less than 1% last month.

Toyota's production in North America dropped by 1%, while car shipments from Japan to its largest market, the United States, also declined by 1%.

The company's global revenue grew 6% in the reported month, supported by a 28% spike in domestic sales and a 2% rise in overseas sales.

U.S. President Donald Trump unveiled on Wednesday a 25% tariff on imported cars and light trucks to take effect on April 3.

Trump's trade policies have sparked global uncertainty, with investors concerned about potential supply chain disruptions, reduced investment, and the looming threat of inflation jeopardizing global economic growth.

In North America, Toyota's sales fell 7%, though the company attributed the decline to fewer operating days and an inventory shortage of hybrids and other vehicles, while noting that demand for its vehicles remained strong.

The production and sales figures include its luxury Lexus brand.

Subsidiary Increases Share Capital in SIAM
data.description:
Singapore Institute of Advanced Medicine or SIAM subsidiary, Proton Therapy, issued 153,175,526 shares at SG$1 per share to the company, according to a filing with the Singapore Exchange on Thursday.

The capital increase was carried out through the capitalization of an existing loan of SG$153.2 million extended to the subsidiary for general working capital purposes.

data.title: India's BSE jumps after markets regulator proposes rejig in derivatives expiry rules
data.description:
Shares of India's BSE jumped nearly 12% on Friday after the country's market regulator proposed rejigging the derivatives expiry schedule, a move analysts said will help ease concerns over the bourse's market share loss.

India's markets regulator, late on Thursday, proposed limiting expiries of all equity derivatives contracts to either Tuesday or Thursday to ensure optimal spacing for expiry dates.

BSE's shares gained as much as 11.7% to 5,232.8 rupees as of 9:51 a.m. IST and were last trading 11.5% higher.

The move secures Tuesday as BSE's expiry day, preventing a potential market share loss had its dominant rival, the National Stock Exchange (NSE), shifted to Monday as previously proposed, attracting more traders and reduced volumes on BSE.

By keeping the separation, BSE will see steady trading activity, reducing the estimated 12% negative impact on its earnings per share, Jefferies analysts said in a note.

data.title: Lens Technology's Profit Climbs 20% in 2024
data.description:
Lens Technology's net profit attributable to shareholders in 2024 climbed 20% to 3.62 billion yuan from 3.02 billion yuan a year ago, according to a Shenzhen Stock Exchange filing on Friday.

Earnings per share in the year rose to 0.73 yuan from 0.61 yuan in 2023.

The Chinese mobile screen maker's operating income jumped 28% year on year to 69.9 billion yuan from 54.5 billion yuan, the filing said.

The company's shares slipped over 1% in recent trade.
